title: [podman-remote] command `ps` and `stop`: ERRO[0000] Could not find flag trace
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
Executing these two commands with every option will print out this error message on the remote node: `ERRO[0000] Could not find flag trace`, although the commands are successfully executed.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On the remote node, execute `ps` and `stop` command with any option. 
for example:
```
 # PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote stop -a                       
```
or
```
# PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote ps -a
```


**Describe the results you received:**
```
# PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote stop -a                       
ERRO[0000] Could not find flag trace
512aa9441caa8ae61fab3fb417d4b26cd1847a6f16c9a743126f68266b7a716b
d93d70b86a2f0bcccca5d78f69dc5aaa7f09cfdbc83054ef842c0ad483ba7e99
```
and
```
PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote ps -a                         
ERRO[0000] Could not find flag trace
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                            COMMAND               CREATED            STATUS                           PORTS  NAMES
d93d70b86a2f  registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rsyslog:latest  /bin/rsyslog.sh       About an hour ago  Exited (0) About a minute ago           rsyslog
512aa9441caa  registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/sadc:latest     /usr/local/bin/sy...  2 hours ago        Exited (137) About a minute ago         sadc
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Don't print out the error line:
```
# PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote stop -a                       
512aa9441caa8ae61fab3fb417d4b26cd1847a6f16c9a743126f68266b7a716b
d93d70b86a2f0bcccca5d78f69dc5aaa7f09cfdbc83054ef842c0ad483ba7e99
```
and
```
PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS="tcp:127.0.0.1:1234" bin/podman-remote ps -a                         
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                            COMMAND               CREATED            STATUS                           PORTS  NAMES
d93d70b86a2f  registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/rsyslog:latest  /bin/rsyslog.sh       About an hour ago  Exited (0) About a minute ago           rsyslog
512aa9441caa  registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7/sadc:latest     /usr/local/bin/sy...  2 hours ago        Exited (137) About a minute ago         sadc
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
On remote node:
```
# podman version                                                                               
Version:            1.3.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.1
Git Commit:         167ce59416f6e03f4477269e33e9e5cf5b700a86
Built:              Mon Apr 15 14:55:33 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: 167ce59416f6e03f4477269e33e9e5cf5b700a86
  go version: go1.12.1
  podman version: 1.3.0-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.7.2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-1.10.3-1.gite558bd5.fc27.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.10.3, commit: 27d18947e07ae525daacba333e67a98a6296fc5d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "27"
  MemFree: 622895104
  MemTotal: 11980427264
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-36.gitad0f525.fc27.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc5+dev
      spec: 1.0.0
  SwapFree: 6043987968
  SwapTotal: 6043987968
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: dhcp-2-7.nay.redhat.com
  kernel: 4.18.7-100.fc27.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 4h 29m 6.67s (Approximately 0.17 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 15
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

comments.body: I pull the latest code, and this issue is not found anymore.
